title: TalosCluster
description: Declarative form of `talosctl cluster create`. Fields under spec map to flags of the chosen provider's subcommand; anything a subcommand does not expose is not modelled here.

patch.description: An inline machine config patch, or "@path" to read one from a file relative to the config file.

cpus.description: Share of host CPUs per node, as a fraction. Maps to --cpus-{controlplanes,workers}.

memory.description: Memory limit per node. A bare number is MiB, matching talosctl's default unit. Maps to --memory-{controlplanes,workers}.

name.description: Cluster name. Maps to --name. Kept short for the qemu provider: it appears twice in the QEMU monitor socket path, which is capped at 107 bytes.

provider.description: Which `talosctl cluster create` subcommand to run. qemu gives real per-node kernels, disks, and upgrades; docker is faster but shares the host kernel.

profile.description: Baseline settings for a throwaway CI cluster. Applied before config-patches, so any single key can be overridden there. Use "none" to apply nothing.

kubernetes-version.description: Kubernetes version to install. Maps to --kubernetes-version; a leading v is stripped, which talosctl requires.

count.description: Number of control plane nodes. Maps to --controlplanes, which only the qemu subcommand exposes; docker always runs exactly one.

cidr.description: CIDR of the cluster network. Maps to --cidr on qemu and --subnet on docker, which set the same underlying option. Nodes are numbered upward from the second address; the first is the gateway.

mtu.description: MTU of the cluster network. Maps to --mtu, which both subcommands accept (it is hidden from --help, not absent); docker applies it to the bridge as com.docker.network.driver.mtu.

config-patches.description: Machine config patches by node role.

cluster.description: Applied to every node. Maps to --config-patch.

controlplanes.description: Applied to control plane nodes. Maps to --config-patch-controlplanes.

workers.description: Applied to worker nodes. Maps to --config-patch-workers.

qemu.description: Settings only `talosctl cluster create qemu` accepts.

talos-version.description: Talos version to boot, with a leading v. Maps to --talos-version.

disks.description: Disks per node, as "driver:size" with optional ":tag=" / ":serial=" parameters. Maps to a single --disks flag. The first disk goes to every node; any after it are attached to workers only.

schematic.description: Image Factory schematic, inline or "@path". Registered with the factory at run time; the returned id is passed as --schematic-id. Mutually exclusive with schematic-id.

schematic-id.description: A pre-registered Image Factory schematic id. Maps to --schematic-id. Mutually exclusive with schematic.

url.description: Image Factory base URL. Maps to --image-factory-url.

auth.description: username:password for the Image Factory. Maps to --image-factory-auth. Pass a secret, never a literal.

presets.description: Boot presets. Maps to --presets. Exactly one of iso, iso-secureboot, pxe, or disk-image must be present. Adding maintenance leaves the nodes unconfigured in maintenance mode: the action then skips the kubeconfig fetch and exports neither KUBECONFIG nor TALOSCONFIG, handing over only the node addresses.

docker.description: Settings only `talosctl cluster create docker` accepts.

image.description: Talos image to run. Maps to --image. This is docker's equivalent of the qemu provider's talos-version.

host-ip.description: Host IP to forward exposed ports to. Maps to --host-ip.

exposed-ports.description: Ports to expose on the init node, as "<hostPort>:<containerPort>/<protocol>". Maps to --exposed-ports.

mounts.description: Mounts to attach to each container, in `docker --mount` syntax. Maps to a repeated --mount.
